question_text: A hydraulic system uses an incompressible liquid to multiply force. If a small piston of area 2 cm² is pushed with a force of 50 N, and the large piston has an area of 50 cm², what is the maximum force that can be exerted on the large piston?
explanation: In a hydraulic system, pressure is transmitted equally throughout an incompressible fluid. First, calculate the pressure on the small piston: pressure = force ÷ area = 50 N ÷ 2 cm² = 25 N/cm². Since pressure is the same everywhere, the force on the large piston is: force = pressure × area = 25 N/cm² × 50 cm² = 1250 N. This demonstrates how hydraulic systems can multiply force.
